name: Json.Decode.NotEmpty
comment:
Functions for encoding not empty collections into JSON values

@docs list, array, dict, set, string

values.name: array
values.comment: Decode a JSON array into a [`NotEmpty.Array`](NotEmpty.Array#Array)

values.name: dict
values.comment:
Decode a JSON object into a [`NotEmpty.Dict`](NotEmpty.Dict#Dict)

    import Json.Decode as D
    import Json.Decode.NotEmpty exposing(dict)

    D.decodeString ( dict D.int ) "{ \"alice\": 42, \"bob\": 99 }" == Ok ( NotEmpty.Dict.fromListWithPair "alice" 42 [ ( "bob", 99 ) ] )
    
    D.decodeString ( dict D.int ) "{}" == Err ...

values.name: list
values.comment:
Decode a JSON array into a [`NotEmpty.List`](NotEmpty.List#List)

    decodeString ( list int ) "[ 1, 2, 3 ]"   == Ok ( NotEmpty.List.withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
    decodeString ( list bool ) "[]"           == Err ...

values.name: set
values.comment: Decode a JSON array into a [`NotEmpty.Set.Set`](NotEmpty.Set#Set)

values.name: string
values.comment: Decode a JSON string into a [`NotEmpty.String.String`](NotEmpty.String#String)

name: Json.Encode.NotEmpty
comment:
Functions for encoding not empty collections into JSON values

@docs list, array, set, dict, string

values.name: array
values.comment: Turn a [`NotEmpty.Array`](NotEmpty.Array#Array) into a JSON array.

values.name: dict
values.comment: Turn a [`NotEmpty.Dict`](NotEmpty.Dict#Dict) into a JSON object.
values.type: (comparable -> String.String) -> (value -> Json.Encode.Value) -> NotEmpty.Dict comparable value -> Json.Encode.Value

values.name: list
values.comment: Turn a [`NotEmpty.List`](NotEmpty.List#List) into a JSON array.

values.name: set
values.comment: Turn a [`NotEmpty.Set.Set`](NotEmpty.Set#Set) into a JSON array.

values.name: string
values.comment: Turn a [`NotEmpty.String`](NotEmpty.String#String) into a JSON array.

name: NotEmpty
comment:
Module with aliases for the different collection types so you can type

    foo : NotEmpty.List Int
instead of

    foo : NotEmpty.List.List Int

@docs List, Array, Dict, Set, String

name: NotEmpty.Array

values.name: append
values.comment: Same as `Array.append`
values.type: NotEmpty.Array.Array a -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a

values.name: appendArray
values.comment:
Append a regular array to a not empty array

    appendArray ( repeat 2 42 ) ( Array.repeat 3 81 ) == fromListWithHead 42 [ 42, 81, 81, 81 ]
values.type: NotEmpty.Array.Array a -> Array.Array a -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a

values.name: filter
values.comment:
Same as `Array.filter` but will return Nothing if no elements satisfy the test

    filter isEven ( fromListWithHead 1 [ 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ] ) == Just ( fromListWithHead 2 [ 4, 6 ] )
    filter isEven ( fromListWithHead 1 [ 3, 5 ] ) == Nothing

values.name: foldl
values.comment: Same as `Array.foldl`
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Same as `Array.foldr`
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a -> b

values.name: fromArray
values.comment:
Convert a regular array into a not empty array, or Nothing if the array is empty

    fromArray ( Array.fromList [] ) == Nothing
    fromArray ( Array.fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ] ) == Just ( fromListWithHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] )

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Same as `Array.fromList` except using a [`NotEmpty.List`](NotEmpty.List#List)

values.name: fromListWithHead
values.comment: Same as [`withHead`](NotEmpty.Array#withHead) but with a list instead of a array

values.name: get
values.comment: Same as `Array.get`

values.name: hasTail
values.comment:
Returns wether the array has a tail with elements

    hasTail ( fromListWithHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == True
    hasTail ( singleton 1 ) == False
    hasTail ( fromListWithHead 1 [] ) == False

values.name: head
values.comment:
Extract the first element from a not empty array.

    head ( fromListWithHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == 1
    head ( singleton 1 ) == 1

values.name: indexedMap
values.comment: Same as `Array.indexedMap`
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> b) -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a -> NotEmpty.Array.Array b

values.name: initialize
values.comment:
Same as `Array.initialize`.

Will always include at least 1 value ( initialized with index 0 ).

    initialize 4 identity    == fromListWithHead 0 [ 1, 2, 3 ]
    initialize 4 ( \n -> n * n ) == fromListWithHead 0 [ 1, 4, 9 ]
    initialize 4 ( always 0 ) == fromListWithHead 0 [ 0, 0, 0 ]
    initialize 0 ( \n -> n + 4 ) == singleton 4

values.name: last
values.comment:
Extract the last element from a not empty array.

    last ( fromListWithHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == 3
    last ( singleton 1 ) == 1

values.name: length
values.comment: Same as `Array.length`

values.name: map
values.comment: Same as `Array.map`

values.name: prependArray
values.comment:
Prepend a regular array to a not empty array

    prependArray ( Array.repeat 2 42 ) ( repeat 3 81 ) == fromListWithHead 42 [ 42, 81, 81, 81 ]
values.type: Array.Array a -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a

values.name: push
values.comment: Same as `Array.push`

values.name: repeat
values.comment:
Same as `Array.repeat`.

Will always include at least 1 value.

    repeat 4 "Value" == fromListWithHead "Value" [ "Value", "Value", "Value" ]
    repeat 0 "Test" == singleton "Test"

values.name: set
values.comment: Same as `Array.set`
values.type: Basics.Int -> a -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a -> NotEmpty.Array.Array a

values.name: singleton
values.comment:
A not empty array containing only the given value.

    toList ( singleton "a" ) == [ "a" ]

values.name: tail
values.comment:
Returns the elements after the first element as a regular array

    tail ( fromListWithHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == Array.fromList [ 2, 3 ]
    tail ( singleton 1 ) == Array.fromList []

values.name: toArray
values.comment:
Convert a not empty array into a regular array

  toArray ( fromListWithHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == Array.fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ]
  toArray ( singleton 1 ) == Array.fromList [ 1 ]

values.name: toIndexedList
values.comment: Same as `Array.toIndexedList`

values.name: toList
values.comment: Same as `Array.toList`

values.name: withHead
values.comment:
A not empty array list with the given head and tail

    array = withHead 5 ( Array.fromList [ 4, 3 ] )
    toArray array == Array.fromList [ 5, 4, 3 ]
    head array == 5
    tail array == Array.fromList [ 4, 3 ]

name: NotEmpty.Dict

values.name: diff
values.comment:
Same as `Dict.diff` but will return Nothing if the resulting dictionary is empty

    diff ( fromListWithPair 1 1 [ ( 2, 2 ), ( 3, 3 ) ] ) ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Just ( fromListWithPair 2 2 [ ( 3, 3 ) ] )
    diff ( singleton 1 1 ) ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Nothing
    diff ( singleton 2 2 ) ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Just ( singleton 2 2 )
values.type: NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable a -> Maybe.Maybe (NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value)

values.name: filter
values.comment:
Same as `Dict.filter` but will return Nothing if no pairs satisfy the test

    filter isKeyEven ( fromListWithPair 1 1 [ ( 2, 2 ), ( 3, 3 ) ] ) == Just ( fromListWithPair 2 2 [] )
    filter isKeyEven ( singleton 1 1 ) == Nothing
values.type: (comparable -> value -> Basics.Bool) -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value -> Maybe.Maybe (NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value)

values.name: foldl
values.comment: Same as `Dict.foldl`
values.type: (key -> value -> b -> b) -> b -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict key value -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Same as `Dict.foldr`
values.type: (key -> value -> b -> b) -> b -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict key value -> b

values.name: fromDict
values.comment:
Convert a regular dict into a not empty dict, or Nothing if the dict is empty

    fromDict ( Dict.fromList [] ) == Nothing
    fromDict ( Dict.fromList [ ( 1, "one" ), ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ] ) == Just ( fromListWithPair 1 "one" [ ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ] )
values.type: Dict.Dict comparable value -> Maybe.Maybe (NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value)

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Same as `Dict.fromList` except using a [`NotEmpty.List`](NotEmpty.List#List)
values.type: NotEmpty.List.List ( comparable, value ) -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value

values.name: fromListWithPair
values.comment: Same as [`withPair`](NotEmpty.Dict#withPair) but with a list or pairs instead of a dict
values.type: comparable -> value -> List.List ( comparable, value ) -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value

values.name: get
values.comment: Same as `Dict.get`
values.type: comparable -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value -> Maybe.Maybe value

values.name: hasTail
values.comment:
Returns wether the dictionary has a tail with elements

    hasTail ( fromListWithPair 1 "one" [ ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ] ) == True
    hasTail ( singleton 1 "one" ) == False
    hasTail ( fromListWithHead 1 "one" [] ) == False

values.name: head
values.comment:
Extract the first pair from a not empty dictionary.

    head ( fromListWithPair 1 "one" [ ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ] ) == ( 1, "one" )
    head ( singleton 1 "one" ) == ( 1, "one" )

values.name: insert
values.comment: Same as `Dict.insert`
values.type: comparable -> value -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value

values.name: intersect
values.comment:
Same as `Dict.intersect` but will return Nothing if the resulting dictionary is empty

    intersect ( fromListWithPair 1 1 [ ( 2, 2 ), ( 3, 3 ) ] ) ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Just ( fromListWithPair 1 1 [] )
    intersect ( singleton 1 1 ) ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Just ( singleton 1 1 )
    intersect ( singleton 2 2 ) ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Nothing
values.type: NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable a -> Maybe.Maybe (NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value)

values.name: last
values.comment:
Extract the last pair from a not empty dictionary.

    last ( fromListWithPair 1 "one" [ ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ] ) == ( 3, "three" )
    last ( singleton 1 "one" ) == ( 1, "one" )

values.name: map
values.comment: Same as `Dict.map`
values.type: (key -> a -> b) -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict key a -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict key b

values.name: member
values.comment: Same as `Dict.member`

values.name: merge
values.comment: Same as `Dict.merge`
values.type: (comparable -> a -> result -> result) -> (comparable -> a -> b -> result -> result) -> (comparable -> b -> result -> result) -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable a -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable b -> result -> result

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Same as `Dict.remove` but will return Nothing if the last value is removed

    remove 1 ( fromListWithPair 1 "one" [ ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ] ) == Just ( fromListWithPair 2 "two" [ ( 3, "three" ) ] )
    remove 1 ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Nothing
    remove 2 ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Just ( singleton 1 "one" )
values.type: comparable -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value -> Maybe.Maybe (NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value)

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create a non empty dictionary with one key-value pair.

values.name: size
values.comment: Same as `Dict.size`

values.name: tail
values.comment:
Returns the elements after the first pair as a regular dictionary

    tail ( fromListWithPair 1 "one" [ ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ] ) == Dict.fromList [ ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ]
    tail ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Dict.fromList []
values.type: NotEmpty.Dict.Dict key value -> Dict.Dict key value

values.name: toDict
values.comment:
Convert a not empty dict into a regular dict

    toDict ( fromListWithPair 1 "one" [ ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ] ) == Dict.fromList [ ( 1, "one" ), ( 2, "two" ), ( 3, "three" ) ]
    toDict ( singleton 1 "one" ) == Dict.fromList [ ( 1, "one" ) ]
values.type: NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value -> Dict.Dict comparable value

values.name: toList
values.comment: Same as `Dict.toList`
values.type: NotEmpty.Dict.Dict key value -> NotEmpty.List.List ( key, value )

values.name: union
values.comment: Same as `Dict.union`
values.type: NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value

values.name: withPair
values.comment:
Create a non empty dictionary from a regular dictionary and a given pair.

In contrast to [`fromDict`](NotEmpty.Dict#fromDict) this will always return a value since there is always at least 1 pair.

    dict = withPair 5 "Five" ( Dict.fromList [ ( 4, "Four" ), ( 3, "Three" ) ] )
    toDict dict == Dict.fromList [ ( 3, "Three" ), ( 4, "Four" ), ( 5, "Five" ) ]
values.type: comparable -> value -> Dict.Dict comparable value -> NotEmpty.Dict.Dict comparable value

name: NotEmpty.List

unions.name: PartitionResult
unions.comment:
Return type for [`NotEmpty.List.partition`](NotEmpty.List#partition)

Will be `NoMatches` when no elements in the list satisfy the test.

Will be `OnlyMatches` when all elements in the list satisfy the test.

Will be `Both` if there a both elements that do and don't satisfy the test.
The first list are the elements that satisfy the test and the second list are the elements that don't.

values.name: all
values.comment: Same as `List.all`

values.name: any
values.comment: Same as `List.any`

values.name: append
values.comment: Same as `List.append`
values.type: NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List a

values.name: appendList
values.comment:
Appends a regular list to a not empty list

    appendList ( singleton 1 ) [ 2, 3 ] == withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ]
    appendList ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) [ 4, 5 ] == withHead 1 [ 2, 3, 4, 5 ]
values.type: NotEmpty.List.List a -> Internal.CoreTypes.CoreList a -> NotEmpty.List.List a

values.name: appendToList
values.comment:
Appends a not empty list to a regular list

    appendToList [ 1, 2 ] ( singleton 3 ) == withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ]
    appendToList [ 1, 2, 3 ] ( withHead 4 [ 5 ] ) == withHead 1 [ 2, 3, 4, 5 ]
    appendToList [] ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ]
values.type: Internal.CoreTypes.CoreList a -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List a

values.name: concat
values.comment: Same as `List.concat`

values.name: concatMap
values.comment: Same as `List.concatMap`
values.type: (a -> NotEmpty.List.List b) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List b

values.name: cons
values.comment:
Add an element to the front of a not empty list.

  cons 1 ( singleton 2 ) == withHead 1 [ 2 ]

values.name: drop
values.comment:
Same as `List.drop` but will always include the last element

    drop 2 ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == withHead 2 [ 3 ]
    drop 0 ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == singleton 3

values.name: filter
values.comment:
Same as `List.filter` but will return Nothing if no elements satisfy the test

    filter isEven ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ] ) == Just ( withHead 2 [ 4, 6 ] )
    filter isEven ( withHead 1 [ 3, 5 ] ) == Nothing

values.name: filterMap
values.comment:
Same as `List.filterMap` but will return Nothing if all elements return Nothing

    numbers1 : Maybe ( List Int )
    numbers1 =
      filterMap String.toInt ( withHead "3" [ "hi", "12", "4th", "May" ] )

    numbers2 : Maybe ( List Int )
    numbers2 =
      filterMap String.toInt ( withHead "hello" [ "world" ] )

    -- numbers1 == Just [3, 12]
    -- numbers2 == Nothing
values.type: (a -> Maybe.Maybe b) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> Maybe.Maybe (NotEmpty.List.List b)

values.name: foldl
values.comment: Same as `List.foldl`
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> b

values.name: foldl1
values.comment:
Same as `List.foldl` but instead of supplying a start value you provide a function that produces the start value based on the first element

  foldl1 (+) negate ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == 4
  foldl1 (::) singleton ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == withHead 3 [ 2, 1 ]
values.type: (a -> acc -> acc) -> (a -> acc) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> acc

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Same as `List.foldr`
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> b

values.name: foldr1
values.comment:
Same as `List.foldl` but instead of supplying a start value you provide a function that produces the start value based on the last element

  foldr1 (+) negate ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == 0
  foldr1 (::) singleton ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ]
values.type: (a -> acc -> acc) -> (a -> acc) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> acc

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Convert a regular list into a not empty list, or Nothing if the list is empty

    fromList [] == Nothing
    fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ] == Just ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] )

values.name: hasTail
values.comment:
Returns wether the list has a tail with elements

    hasTail ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == True
    hasTail ( singleton 1 ) == False
    hasTail ( withHead 1 [] ) == False

values.name: head
values.comment: Same as `List.head` but will always always return a value

values.name: indexedMap
values.comment: Same as `List.indexedMap`
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> b) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List b

values.name: intersperse
values.comment: Same as `List.intersperse`

values.name: last
values.comment:
Extract the last element from a not empty list.

    last ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == 3
    last ( singleton 1 ) == 1

values.name: length
values.comment: Same as `List.length`

values.name: map
values.comment: Same as `List.map`

values.name: map2
values.comment: Same as `List.map2`
values.type: (a -> b -> result) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List b -> NotEmpty.List.List result

values.name: map3
values.comment: Same as `List.map3`
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> result) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List b -> NotEmpty.List.List c -> NotEmpty.List.List result

values.name: map4
values.comment: Same as `List.map4`
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> result) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List b -> NotEmpty.List.List c -> NotEmpty.List.List d -> NotEmpty.List.List result

values.name: map5
values.comment: Same as `List.map5`
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> result) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List b -> NotEmpty.List.List c -> NotEmpty.List.List d -> NotEmpty.List.List e -> NotEmpty.List.List result

values.name: maximum
values.comment: Same as `List.maximum` but will always return a value

values.name: member
values.comment: Same as `List.member`

values.name: minimum
values.comment: Same as `List.minimum` but will always return a value

values.name: partition
values.comment: Same as `List.partition` but will return a [`PartitionResult`](NotEmpty.List#PartitionResult) since either but not both lists could be empty.

values.name: product
values.comment: Same as `List.product`

values.name: range
values.comment:
Create a list of numbers, every element increasing by one.
You give the lowest and highest number that should be in the list.

Will always include the low value.

    toList ( range 3 6 ) == [ 3, 4, 5, 6 ]
    toList ( range 3 3 ) == [ 3 ]
    toList ( range 6 3 ) == [ 6 ]

values.name: repeat
values.comment:
Create a not empty list with *n* copies of a value.

Will always include at least 1 value.

    toList ( repeat 4 "Value" ) == [ "Value", "Value", "Value", "Value" ]
    toList ( repeat 0 "Test" ) == [ "Test" ]

values.name: reverse
values.comment: Same as `List.reverse`

values.name: singleton
values.comment:
A not empty list containing only the given value.

    toList ( singleton "a" ) == [ "a" ]

values.name: sort
values.comment: Same as `List.sort`

values.name: sortBy
values.comment: Same as `List.sortBy`

values.name: sortWith
values.comment: Same as `List.sortWith`
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> NotEmpty.List.List a -> NotEmpty.List.List a

values.name: sum
values.comment: Same as `List.sum`

values.name: tail
values.comment:
Same as `List.tail` but will always return a value

    tail ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == [ 2, 3 ]
    tail ( singleton 1 ) == []

values.name: take
values.comment:
Same as `List.take` but will always include the first element

    take 2 ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == withHead 1 [ 2 ]
    take 0 ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == singleton 1

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Convert a not empty list into a regular list

  toList ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == [ 1, 2, 3 ]
  toList ( singleton 1 ) == [ 1 ]

values.name: unCons
values.comment:
Deconstruct a not empty list into it's head and tail

    unCons ( singleton 5 ) = ( 5, [] )
    unCons ( withHead 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) = ( 1, [ 2, 3 ] )

values.name: unzip
values.comment: Same as `List.unzip`
values.type: NotEmpty.List.List ( a, b ) -> ( NotEmpty.List.List a, NotEmpty.List.List b )

values.name: withHead
values.comment:
A not empty list with the given head and tail

    ls = withHead 5 [ 4, 3 ]
    toList ls == [ 5, 4, 3 ]
    head ls == 5
    tail ls == [ 4, 3 ]

name: NotEmpty.Set

values.name: diff
values.comment:
Same as `Set.diff` but will return Nothing if the resulting set is empty

    diff ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) ( singleton 1 ) == Just ( fromListWithValue 2 2 [ ( 3, 3 ) ] )
    diff ( singleton 1 ) ( singleton 1 ) == Nothing
    diff ( singleton 2 ) ( singleton 1 ) == Just ( singleton 2 )
values.type: NotEmpty.Set.Set comparable -> NotEmpty.Set.Set comparable -> Maybe.Maybe (NotEmpty.Set.Set comparable)

values.name: filter
values.comment:
Same as `Set.filter` but will return Nothing if no values satisfy the test

    filter isKeyEven ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == Just ( fromListWithValue 2 [] )
    filter isKeyEven ( singleton 1 ) == Nothing

values.name: foldl
values.comment: Same as `Set.foldl`
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> NotEmpty.Set.Set a -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Same as `Set.foldr`
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> NotEmpty.Set.Set a -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Same as `Set.fromList` except using a [`NotEmpty.List`](NotEmpty.List#List)

values.name: fromListWithValue
values.comment: Same as [`withValue`](NotEmpty.Set#withValue) but with a list instead of a set

values.name: fromSet
values.comment:
Convert a regular set into a not empty set, or Nothing if the set is empty

    fromSet ( Set.fromList [] ) == Nothing
    fromSet ( Set.fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ] ) == Just ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] )

values.name: hasTail
values.comment:
Returns wether the set has a tail with elements

    hasTail ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == True
    hasTail ( singleton 1 ) == False
    hasTail ( fromListWithHead 1 [] ) == False

values.name: head
values.comment:
Extract the first value from a not empty set.

    head ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == 1
    head ( singleton 1 ) == 1

values.name: insert
values.comment: Same as `Set.insert`

values.name: intersect
values.comment:
Same as `Set.intersect` but will return Nothing if the resulting set is empty

    intersect ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) ( singleton 1 ) == Just ( fromListWithValue 1 1 [] )
    intersect ( singleton 1 ) ( singleton 1 ) == Just ( singleton 1 )
    intersect ( singleton 2 ) ( singleton 1 ) == Nothing
values.type: NotEmpty.Set.Set comparable -> NotEmpty.Set.Set comparable -> Maybe.Maybe (NotEmpty.Set.Set comparable)

values.name: last
values.comment:
Extract the last value from a not empty set.

    head ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == 3
    head ( singleton 1 ) == 1

values.name: map
values.comment: Same as `Set.map`

values.name: member
values.comment: Same as `Set.member`

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Same as `Set.remove` but will return Nothing if the last value is removed

    remove 1 ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == Just ( fromListWithValue 2 [ 3 ] )
    remove 1 ( singleton 1 ) == Nothing
    remove 2 ( singleton 1 ) == Just ( singleton 1 )

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create a non empty set with one value.

values.name: size
values.comment: Same as `Set.size`

values.name: tail
values.comment:
Returns the elements after the first value as a regular set

    tail ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == Set.fromList [ 2, 3 ]
    tail ( singleton 1 ) == Set.fromList []

values.name: toList
values.comment: Same as `Set.toList`

values.name: toSet
values.comment:
Convert a not empty set into a regular set

    toSet ( fromListWithValue 1 [ 2, 3 ] ) == Set.fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ]
    toSet ( singleton 1 ) == Set.singleton 1

values.name: union
values.comment: Same as `Set.union`
values.type: NotEmpty.Set.Set comparable -> NotEmpty.Set.Set comparable -> NotEmpty.Set.Set comparable

values.name: withValue
values.comment:
Create a non empty set from a regular set and a given value.

In contrast to [`fromSet`](NotEmpty.Set#fromSet) this will always return a value since there is always at least 1 value.

    set = withValue 5 ( Set.fromList [ 4, 3 ] )
    toSet set == Set.fromList [ 3, 4, 5 ]

name: NotEmpty.String

values.name: all
values.comment: Same as String.all

values.name: any
values.comment: Same as String.any

values.comment: Same as String.append

values.comment: Append a regular string to a not empty string

values.name: appendToString
values.comment: Append a not empty string to a regular string

values.name: concat
values.comment: Same as String.concat

values.comment: Same as String.fromInt

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Same as String.fromList, but takes a not empty list

values.name: fromString
values.comment:
Convert a regular string into a not empty string, or Nothing if the string is empty

    fromString "" == Nothing
    fromString "hello" == Just ( consString 'h' "ello" )

values.name: head
values.comment:
Get the first character from a not empty string

  head ( consString 'h' "ello" ) == 'h'

values.comment: Same as String.indexes

values.name: indices
values.comment: Alias for `indexes`.

values.comment: Same as String.join

values.comment: Same as String.left but will take at least 1 character

values.name: startsWith

values.comment:
Get the tail from a not empty string

  tail ( consString 'h' "ello" ) == "ello"

values.name: toString
values.comment:
Convert a not empty string into a regular string

  toString ( consString 'h' "ello" ) == "hello"
  toString ( consString 'a' "" ) == "a"
